items.name: AddAssign
items.doc: Protocol for types that support addition assignment (+=).

members.name: addAssign
members.doc: Adds the other value to this one in place.

items.name: Addable
items.doc:
Protocol for types that support addition (+).
The Output type allows the result to differ from the operand types.

members.name: zero
members.doc:
The additive identity (zero).
Used as the starting value for sum operations.

items.name: And
items.doc:
Protocol for types that support logical AND (and keyword).
Uses short-circuit evaluation: the right operand is only evaluated if needed.

members.name: logicalAnd
members.doc:
Performs logical AND with short-circuit evaluation.
The closure is only called if self is truthy.

items.name: ArrayMatchable
items.doc:
Protocol for types that support array pattern matching.
Enables patterns like `[a, b]`, `[a, ..rest]`, `[a, .., z]`, `[a, ..rest, z]`

The protocol provides methods for length checking, element access, and slicing.
Conforming types include Array[T] and Slice[T].

members.name: matchGet
members.doc:
Returns the element at the given index.
Caller guarantees index is valid (0 <= index < matchLength()).

members.name: matchLength
members.doc: Returns the number of elements in the collection.

members.name: matchSlice
members.signature: func matchSlice(from: Int64, to: Int64) -> Slice[Element
members.doc:
Returns a slice from `from` (inclusive) to `to` (exclusive).
Caller guarantees valid bounds (0 <= from <= to <= matchLength()).

items.name: BitwiseAnd
items.doc: Protocol for types that support bitwise AND (&).

members.name: bitwiseAnd
members.doc: Performs bitwise AND of this value with another.

items.name: BitwiseAndAssign
items.doc: Protocol for types that support bitwise AND assignment (&=).

members.name: bitwiseAndAssign
members.doc: Performs bitwise AND with the other value in place.

items.name: BitwiseNot
items.doc: Protocol for types that support bitwise NOT (~).

members.name: bitwiseNot
members.doc: Returns the bitwise complement of this value.

items.name: BitwiseOr
items.doc: Protocol for types that support bitwise OR (|).

members.name: bitwiseOr
members.doc: Performs bitwise OR of this value with another.

items.name: BitwiseOrAssign
items.doc: Protocol for types that support bitwise OR assignment (|=).

members.name: bitwiseOrAssign
members.doc: Performs bitwise OR with the other value in place.

items.name: BitwiseXor
items.doc: Protocol for types that support bitwise XOR (^).

members.name: bitwiseXor
members.doc: Performs bitwise XOR of this value with another.

items.name: BitwiseXorAssign
items.doc: Protocol for types that support bitwise XOR assignment (^=).

members.name: bitwiseXorAssign
members.doc: Performs bitwise XOR with the other value in place.

items.name: Bool
items.signature:
public struct Bool:
    Equatable,
    Matchable,
    Formattable,
    Hash,
    And[Bool],
    Or[Bool],
    Not,
    ExpressibleByBoolLiteral,
    BooleanConditional,
    FFISafe
items.doc:
The boolean type with support for logical operations, equality, hashing, and formatting.
Bool conforms to Equatable, Matchable, Formattable, Hash, And, Or, Not,
ExpressibleByBoolLiteral, BooleanConditional, and FFISafe protocols.

members.name: boolValue
members.doc: Returns the underlying boolean value for use in conditionals.

members.name: equals
members.doc:
Compares this Bool with another for equality.
Returns true if both values are the same.

members.name: format
members.signature: public func format(options: FormatOptions = FormatOptions.default()) -> String
members.doc:
Formats this Bool as a string.
Default: "true" or "false".
Debug: "Bool(true)" or "Bool(false)".

members.name: hash
members.signature: public func hash[H](mutating into hasher: H) where H: Hasher
members.doc: Hashes this Bool value into the given hasher.

members.name: init
members.doc: Creates a Bool from a boolean literal value.

members.name: logicalAnd
members.doc:
Logical AND with short-circuit evaluation.
The closure is only evaluated if self is true.

members.name: logicalNot
members.doc: Logical NOT - returns the inverse of this boolean.

members.name: logicalOr
members.doc:
Logical OR with short-circuit evaluation.
The closure is only evaluated if self is false.

members.name: matches
members.doc:
Matches this Bool against another in pattern matching contexts.
Returns true if both values are the same.

items.name: BooleanConditional
items.doc:
Protocol for types that can be used as boolean conditions in if/while statements.
Types conforming to this protocol can be used directly in conditionals.

members.name: boolValue
members.doc: Returns the boolean value for use in conditionals.

items.name: BooleanLiteralType
items.doc: The default type for boolean literals when type cannot be inferred.

items.name: CharLiteralType
items.doc: The default type for character literals when type cannot be inferred.

items.name: Cloneable
items.doc:
Protocol for types that can be copied via a clone() method.
Unlike simple Copyable (bitwise copy), Cloneable types have custom copy behavior.
When a Cloneable value is copied, clone() is called automatically.
Cloneable extends Copyable, allowing cloneable types to be used where copyable is required.

members.name: clone
members.doc:
Creates and returns a copy of this value.
The implementation defines how deep the copy is.

items.name: ClosedRange
items.signature: public struct ClosedRange[T]: Equatable, Iterable where T: Steppable, T: Comparable
items.doc:
A closed range [start, end] that includes both endpoints.
Created with the ..= operator. Example: 0..=10 includes 0 through 10.

members.name: contains
members.doc: Returns true if the value is within the range [start, end].

members.name: end
members.doc: The upper bound of the range (inclusive).

members.name: equals
members.doc: Compares this range with another for equality.

members.name: init
members.doc: Creates a range from start to end (both inclusive).

members.name: isEmpty
members.doc: Returns true if the range contains no elements (start > end).

members.name: iter
members.doc: Returns an iterator over the range values.

items.name: ClosedRangeConstructible
items.doc:
Protocol for types that support the inclusive range operator (..=).
Example: 0..=10 creates a range from 0 to 10 (inclusive).

members.name: inclusiveRange
members.doc: Creates an inclusive range from this value to the end value.

items.name: ClosedRangeIterator
items.signature: public struct ClosedRangeIterator[T]: Iterator where T: Steppable, T: Comparable
items.doc: Iterator for ClosedRange that yields values from start through end (inclusive).

members.name: init
members.signature: public init(current current: T, end end: T, finished finished: Bool)
members.doc: Creates an iterator starting at current and ending at end (inclusive).

members.name: next
members.doc: Returns the next value in the range, or None if exhausted.

items.name: Coalesce
items.doc:
Protocol for types that support the null coalescing operator (??).
Enables providing a default value when the receiver is None/null.
Uses short-circuit evaluation: the default is only computed if needed.

members.name: coalesce
members.doc:
Returns the contained value if present, otherwise evaluates and returns the default.
The closure is only called if self is None/null.

items.name: Comparable
items.doc:
Protocol for types that have a total ordering.
Extends Equatable to add comparison capabilities.

members.name: compare
members.doc: Compares this value with another and returns their ordering.

items.name: ControlFlow
items.doc:
The fundamental type for early return semantics.
Used internally by the try operator and error propagation.
Continue indicates normal flow with the contained value.
Break indicates early return/error with the contained value.

items.name: Convertible
items.doc:
Protocol for types that can be constructed from another type.
Implement this to enable explicit type conversions using the init(from:) pattern.

members.name: init
members.doc: Creates an instance of this type from a value of another type.

items.name: Copyable
items.doc:
Marker protocol for types that can be implicitly copied (bitwise copy).
Types implicitly conform to Copyable unless opted out with `not Copyable`.
Bitwise copy means the type's memory is directly duplicated without any special handling.

items.name: Defaultable
items.doc:
Protocol for types that have a default value.
Enables construction without arguments via init().

members.name: init
members.doc: Creates an instance with the default value.

items.name: DictionaryLiteralType
items.signature: public type DictionaryLiteralType[K, V] = std.collections.Dictionary[K, V, std.collections.DefaultHasher

items.name: DivideAssign
items.doc: Protocol for types that support division assignment (/=).

members.name: divideAssign
members.doc: Divides this value by the other in place.

items.name: Divisible
items.doc: Protocol for types that support division (/).

members.name: divide
members.doc: Divides this value by another and returns the result.

items.name: Equal
items.doc: Protocol for types that support equality comparison (==).

members.name: equals
members.doc: Compares this value with another for equality.

items.name: Equatable
items.doc:
Protocol for types that can be compared for equality.
Provides the foundation for the == operator via the Equal protocol extension.

members.name: equals
members.doc: Returns true if this value equals the other.

items.name: ExpressibleByArrayLiteral
items.doc:
User-facing protocol for array literal initialization.
Provides a more convenient interface using LiteralSlice.
The compiler only uses `_ExpressibleByArrayLiteral` directly; this is a
convenience wrapper a type may also adopt to take `LiteralSlice` arguments.

members.name: init
members.doc: Creates an instance from a literal slice of elements.

items.name: ExpressibleByBoolLiteral
items.doc: Protocol for types that can be constructed from boolean literals (true/false).

members.name: init
members.doc: Creates an instance from a boolean literal.

items.name: ExpressibleByCharLiteral
items.doc: Protocol for types that can be constructed from character literals.

members.name: init
members.doc: Creates an instance from a character literal.

items.name: ExpressibleByDictionaryLiteral
items.doc:
User-facing protocol for dictionary literal initialization.
Provides a more convenient interface using LiteralSlice.
The compiler only uses `_ExpressibleByDictionaryLiteral` directly; this is
a convenience wrapper a type may also adopt to take `LiteralSlice` arguments.

members.name: init
members.doc: Creates an instance from a literal slice of key-value pairs.

items.name: ExpressibleByFloatLiteral
items.doc: Protocol for types that can be constructed from floating-point literals.

members.name: init
members.doc: Creates an instance from a floating-point literal.

items.name: ExpressibleByIntLiteral
items.doc: Protocol for types that can be constructed from integer literals.

members.name: init
members.doc: Creates an instance from an integer literal.

items.name: ExpressibleByNullLiteral
items.doc: Protocol for types that can be constructed from null literals.

members.name: init
members.doc: Creates a null/none instance.

items.name: ExpressibleByStringLiteral
items.doc: Protocol for types that can be constructed from string literals.

members.name: init
members.doc: Creates an instance from a string literal.

items.name: FloatLiteralType
items.doc: The default type for floating-point literals when type cannot be inferred.

items.name: FromResidual
items.doc:
Protocol that enables early return propagation.
Types conforming to FromResidual can be constructed from an early return value,
allowing error types to propagate through function boundaries.

members.name: fromResidual
members.doc: Creates an instance from an early return/error value.

items.name: Greater
items.doc: Protocol for types that support greater-than comparison (>).

members.name: greaterThan
members.doc: Returns true if this value is greater than the other.

items.name: GreaterOrEqual
items.doc: Protocol for types that support greater-than-or-equal comparison (>=).

members.name: greaterThanOrEqual
members.doc: Returns true if this value is greater than or equal to the other.

items.name: Hash
items.doc:
Protocol for types that can be hashed.
Hashable types must also be equatable (equal values must have equal hashes).

members.name: hash
members.signature: func hash[H](mutating into hasher: H) where H: Hasher
members.doc: Feeds this value's bytes into the given hasher.

items.name: Hasher
items.doc:
Protocol for hash algorithm implementations.
Used by Hash-conforming types to compute hash values.

members.name: finish
members.doc: Finalizes the hash computation and returns the hash value.

members.name: write
members.doc: Writes bytes to the hasher state.

items.name: IntegerLiteralType
items.doc: The default type for integer literals when type cannot be inferred.

items.name: LeftShift
items.doc:
Protocol for types that support left bit shift (<<).
Default Rhs is lang.i64 because type defaults must be resolvable at parse time.

members.name: shiftLeft
members.doc: Shifts this value's bits left by the given count.

items.name: LeftShiftAssign
items.doc: Protocol for types that support left shift assignment (<<=).

members.name: shiftLeftAssign
members.doc: Shifts this value's bits left by the given count in place.

items.name: Less
items.doc: Protocol for types that support less-than comparison (<).

members.name: lessThan
members.doc: Returns true if this value is less than the other.

items.name: LessOrEqual
items.doc: Protocol for types that support less-than-or-equal comparison (<=).

members.name: lessThanOrEqual
members.doc: Returns true if this value is less than or equal to the other.

items.name: Matchable
items.doc:
Protocol for types that can be matched in match expressions.
Enables pattern matching support for custom types.

members.name: matches
members.doc: Returns true if this value matches the pattern.

items.name: Modulo
items.doc: Protocol for types that support modulo/remainder (%).

members.name: modulo
members.doc: Computes the remainder of dividing this value by another.

items.name: ModuloAssign
items.doc: Protocol for types that support modulo assignment (%=).

members.name: modAssign
members.doc: Computes the remainder of this value divided by the other in place.

items.name: Multipliable
items.doc: Protocol for types that support multiplication (*).

members.name: one
members.doc:
The multiplicative identity (one).
Used as the starting value for product operations.

items.name: MultiplyAssign
items.doc: Protocol for types that support multiplication assignment (*=).

members.name: multiplyAssign
members.doc: Multiplies this value by the other in place.

items.name: Negatable
items.doc: Protocol for types that support unary negation (-x).

members.name: negate
members.doc: Returns the negated value.

items.name: Not
items.doc: Protocol for types that support logical NOT (not keyword).

members.name: logicalNot
members.doc: Returns the logical negation of this value.

items.name: NotEqual
items.doc: Protocol for types that support inequality comparison (!=).

members.name: notEquals
members.doc: Compares this value with another for inequality.

items.name: NullLiteralType
items.doc: The default type for null literals when type cannot be inferred.

items.name: Or
items.doc:
Protocol for types that support logical OR (or keyword).
Uses short-circuit evaluation: the right operand is only evaluated if needed.

members.name: logicalOr
members.doc:
Performs logical OR with short-circuit evaluation.
The closure is only called if self is falsy.

items.name: Ordering
items.doc:
Represents the result of comparing two values.
Used by the Comparable protocol to express total ordering.

members.name: Equal
members.doc: The values are equal.

members.name: Greater
members.doc: The first value is greater than the second.

members.name: Less
members.doc: The first value is less than the second.

members.name: equals
members.doc: Compares this ordering with another for equality.

members.name: format
members.signature: public func format(options: FormatOptions = FormatOptions.default()) -> String
members.doc: Formats this ordering as a string.

members.name: notEquals
members.doc: Compares this ordering with another for inequality.

members.name: reverse
members.doc: Reverses this ordering (Less becomes Greater and vice versa).

members.name: then
members.doc:
Returns this ordering if not Equal, otherwise returns the other ordering.
Useful for chaining comparisons by multiple fields.

members.name: thenWith
members.doc:
Returns this ordering if not Equal, otherwise evaluates and returns the comparison.
Lazy version of then() that only evaluates the closure if needed.

items.name: Range
items.signature: public struct Range[T]: Equatable, Iterable where T: Steppable, T: Comparable
items.doc:
A half-open range [start, end) that excludes the end value.
Created with the ..< operator. Example: 0..<10 includes 0 through 9.

members.name: contains
members.doc: Returns true if the value is within the range [start, end).

members.name: end
members.doc: The upper bound of the range (exclusive).

members.name: equals
members.doc: Compares this range with another for equality.

members.name: init
members.doc: Creates a range from start (inclusive) to end (exclusive).

members.name: isEmpty
members.doc: Returns true if the range contains no elements (start >= end).

members.name: iter
members.doc: Returns an iterator over the range values.

items.name: RangeConstructible
items.doc:
Protocol for types that support the exclusive range operator (..<).
Example: 0..<10 creates a range from 0 to 9.

members.name: exclusiveRange
members.doc: Creates an exclusive range from this value to the end value.

items.name: RangeIterator
items.signature: public struct RangeIterator[T]: Iterator where T: Steppable, T: Comparable
items.doc: Iterator for Range that yields values from start up to (but not including) end.

members.name: init
members.signature: public init(current current: T, end end: T)
members.doc: Creates an iterator starting at current and ending before end.

members.name: next
members.doc: Returns the next value in the range, or None if exhausted.

items.name: RangeMatchable
items.doc:
Protocol for types that support range pattern matching.
Enables patterns like `start..=end`, `start..<end`, `..=end`, `..<end`, `start..`

The Bound type parameter allows heterogeneous matching, e.g., matching
an Int64 against Char bounds.

members.name: isAtLeast
members.doc: Returns true if self >= bound (for start.. patterns)

members.name: isAtMost
members.doc: Returns true if self <= bound (for ..=end patterns)

members.name: isBelow
members.doc: Returns true if self < bound (for ..<end patterns)

items.name: RightShift
items.doc:
Protocol for types that support right bit shift (>>).
Default Rhs is lang.i64 because type defaults must be resolvable at parse time.

members.name: shiftRight
members.doc: Shifts this value's bits right by the given count.

items.name: RightShiftAssign
items.doc: Protocol for types that support right shift assignment (>>=).

members.name: shiftRightAssign
members.doc: Shifts this value's bits right by the given count in place.

items.name: StringLiteralType
items.doc: The default type for string literals when type cannot be inferred.

items.name: SubtractAssign
items.doc: Protocol for types that support subtraction assignment (-=).

members.name: subtractAssign
members.doc: Subtracts the other value from this one in place.

items.name: Subtractable
items.doc: Protocol for types that support subtraction (-).

members.name: subtract
members.doc: Subtracts another value from this one and returns the result.

items.name: Tryable
items.doc:
Protocol that enables the `try expr` syntax.
Types conforming to Tryable can be used with the try operator,
which extracts the success value or propagates the error.

members.name: Early
members.doc: The type propagated on failure.

members.name: Output
members.doc: The type produced on success.

members.name: tryExtract
members.doc:
Extracts the value or signals early return.
Returns Continue(value) on success or Break(early) on failure.

items.name: _ExpressibleByArrayLiteral
items.doc:
Low-level protocol for array literal initialization.
The compiler calls this directly with raw pointer and count.

members.name: init
members.doc: Creates an instance from a raw pointer to elements and count.

items.name: _ExpressibleByDictionaryLiteral
items.doc:
Low-level protocol for dictionary literal initialization.
The compiler calls this directly with raw pointer and count.

members.name: init
members.doc: Creates an instance from a raw pointer to key-value pairs and count.

items.name: fatalError
items.doc: Abort the process with a message.
